Strip_Summary: Close-up: scar on stickman torso. Long curved line pulled from under ribs. Worn fabric shirt edge. Grey vignette.
Stickman pulling shirt down over scar. Automatic gesture. Done so many times hand doesn't think anymore.
Morning routine: checking scar hidden before leaving house. Someone standing behind in line - same gesture.
Shirt fabric catching on raised scar edge. Small pull. Like thread snagging. Stickman not flinching anymore.
Bold text on dark: 'Some people have birthmarks. You have this.'
Bold text on dark: 'They said it grows back. So you believed them.'
Dry cracked field stretching to horizon. Stickman walking barefoot into barren earth every morning. Routine.
Soil cracking under bare feet. Dry earth breaking apart. Dust rising from each step in empty landscape.
Stickman crouching, pressing thumb into dirt. Turns to dust between fingers. Nothing holds. Empty field.
Small child stickman sitting on dirt floor inside house. Not playing. Not crying. Just sitting like a thing to do.
Stickman not knowing when child stopped asking for food. But child stopped. Silent acceptance of hunger.
Interior: counting jar contents. Rice. Salt. Small bag of dried something. Three days. Maybe four if skipping.
Stickman at empty spot. Been skipping meals for a while. Stomach doesn't complain anymore. Adjusted to nothing.
Bold text on dark: 'You don't remember when that started.'
Faceless figure standing at yard edge. Motionless. Not knocking. Just standing and waiting. Stickman inside.
Debt collector at fence edge. Not close enough to threaten. Not far enough to ignore. Part of the fence.
Stickman in doorway watching. Doesn't have it. Didn't have it last week. Hands wet from washing rice. Wiping on shirt.
Debt collector leaving when sun passes corner tree. Every Tuesday like clockwork. Watched from doorway.
Stickman watching figure go. Hands still wet from rice. Wiping them on shirt twice. Nervous repetition.
Bold text on dark: 'He'll be back.'
Village scene: neighbor returned after five days gone. Nobody asked where. People leave and come back here.
Nobody counts days in this village. People leave and come back. But this time - he came back with money.
Neighbor with money: not a lot but more than anyone has seen. New sheets for roof. Wife got sandals.
New roof sheets and plastic strap sandals. Small purchases that mark sudden money in poverty village.
Someone asked how. Neighbor said a word heard before but never paid attention to. The word passing between lips.
Neighbor touching own side while saying it. Casually. Like adjusting shirt. Same gesture Subject knows intimately.
The phrase spreading: 'It grows back.' Repeated like fact. Three words carrying weight of desperation.
Bold text on dark: 'He said it like he was talking about hair.'
Village well: women talking. Someone's cousin went to city. Week gone. Came back fine. Walking, talking, working.
The number always the same: five thousand. Consistent price across every telling. No matter who says it.
Came back fine, everyone says. The number fixed in village consciousness like a crop price. Background knowledge.
Night: stickman doing math against debt. Child sleeping beside. Not enough to cover all. But enough to stop Tuesdays.
Maybe. That one word. Stickman lying in dark doing math that trades body for debt relief. Uncertain arithmetic.
Dark room: child breathing small steady breaths. Stickman touching own side under blanket. Feeling what's still there.
Close-up: hand under blanket touching own side. Feeling kidney still inside. Just once. Cool blue wash edges.
Skipping dinner. Not decision - stomach closed itself. Body already preparing for what mind hasn't said yes to.
POV: table surface. Folded paper appearing. Handwritten number in pencil on receipt back. No name, no instructions.
Just the number. Pencil smudged where someone's thumb pressed. Paper folded. Placed under salt jar. Kept.
Stickman at table, fingers smelling like pencil lead. Paper under jar where salt lives. Not thrown away.
Bold text on dark: 'You don't throw it away.'
Empty chair outside house two doors down. Woman gone. Daughter says visiting family. But goat tied unfed.
Goat's rope fraying from pulling against post. Nobody fed it. Chair empty. Everyone knows but nobody says.
Nobody asks where she really went. Already know. Or think they do. Village silence about familiar absence.
Night: paper from under salt jar. Child coughing in other room. Dry cough shaking something loose inside.
Bold text on dark: 'That's it. That's the whole thing.'
Night bus. Stickman by window seat. Plastic bag on lap with everything: clothes, ID, thirty in cash.
Seat warm from previous person. Vinyl cracking as stickman presses back. Sitting where someone else sat before.
Village disappearing behind dust through bus window. Last house lights blinking once. Then nothing ahead.
Road smoother after first hour. Stickman touching own side through shirt. Just once. Knowing it's still there.
Body still whole. Stickman keeps thinking it. The whole ride. Can't stop the thought or it might not be true.
Bold text on dark: 'Your body is still whole. You keep thinking that.'
Small room: four plastic chairs. Fluorescent light. No windows. Three faceless stickmen sitting apart. Silence.
Nobody talks. Nobody looks at each other. All know why. Nobody says it. Nobody asks anything here.
Stickman realizes he isn't asking either. Conforming to room's silent rules. Not questioning the silence.
One person touching their side repeatedly. Same spot. Checking. Stickman looks away at crack in floor tile.
Crack running through floor tile. Staring at that instead. Safer than looking at mirror of own future.
Name called. Figure stands, walks through door at end. Door closes. Nothing heard after. Silence returns.
Man beside rubbing hands together. Stickman mimicking without meaning to. Contagious anxiety spreading.
Close-up: jaw tightening. Not sure when it started. Hands in lap. Waiting. Cold grey vignette at edges.
Name or number called. Stickman stands. Light turns on - white, too white, everything humming. Transition.
POV: lying down. Don't remember getting on table. Something cold on arm. Strap. Band. Surgical light above.
Chemical smell. Sharp. Like cleaning fluid mixed with metal. Stickman lying flat. Preparation happening around.
Shapes around - masks, gloved hands. No faces visible. Just system delivering procedure. Faceless operators.
The question asked to the ceiling: how long until it grows back. Held since the village. Asked at last moment.
No answer. The question hanging in chemical air. Same question neighbor answered casually two months ago.
Bold text on dark: 'How long until it grows back.'
No one answers. Count backward from ten. Gets to seven. Or six. Can't remember which. Fading.
Light getting softer. Then gone. Consciousness fading to black. Surgery gap - no image, just absence.
Waking up. Trying to sit up. Body doesn't follow for one second. Yellow stained ceiling above. Grey vignette.
Yellow stained ceiling. Still fan that doesn't turn. Different room than before. Cheap hotel room emerging.
Thin mattress. Thin blanket. Plastic bag of pills on small table. Warm still air. No window opened.
Air warm and still like nobody opened window. Stale cheap room. Recovery space without any care.
Side hurts. Not sharp - deep pull. Like stitched too tight. Stickman pressing hand flat against bandaged side.
Gauze. Tape. Warm to touch. Feeling through dressing. Something missing beneath. New geography of body.
Something different inside and cannot name it. Lighter. That is the closest word. Grey vignette slowly.
Bold text on dark: 'That's the closest you get.'
Trying to stand. Legs work but balance wrong. Center of gravity shifted to one side. Hand reaching for wall.
Fingers pressed flat against cold plaster wall. Bracing. Body leaning wrong. New asymmetry in posture.
Trying to remember what before felt like. When everything was still inside. Can't. Grey vignette edges.
Bold text on dark: 'Not the feeling. Not the weight. Just gone.'
Thin envelope on table next to pills. Hands opening it. Counting bills. Counting again. Then stopping. Not enough.
Not enough. Phone dialed. Nothing. Dialed again. Same nothing. Number doesn't connect. System abandoned Subject.
Thinking about neighbor's words. Waiting for them to feel true. It does not arrive. Stickman on hotel bed.
Door opening in next room. Footsteps. Someone new being walked in. Chair scraping floor. Then quiet. Cycle.
Stickman lying back down. Water stain on ceiling shaped like nothing. Staring because nothing else to stare at.
Wondering if person next door asked same question. If anyone answered them. Same system, different body.
Wondering if answered. Then legs moving toward door before deciding to leave. Body choosing before mind does.
Feet walking to door. Decision made by body, not brain. Leaving cheap hotel recovery room. Walking out.
Coming home. Bus ride long. Every bump finding stitches. Road sending pain through side. Arriving at village.
Child running to stickman at bus stop. Picking child up. Side pulling sharp with pain. Holding anyway.
Holding child despite pain. Not yet. Not letting go yet. Arms around small body. Stitches pulling but holding.
Tuesday: debt collector taking money. Writing in book. Closing it. Half the debt paid. Half still remaining.
Bold text on dark: 'He'll be back. You knew that before you got on the bus.'
Back in field. Trying to lift what used to carry one-handed. Hands shaking. Cannot grip. Body changed.
Back won't straighten. Setting weight down. Trying again. Same failure. Body fighting on every movement.
Body fighting every time now. New limitation. What was once natural labor now impossible. Permanent cost.
Everything blurring for moment - dirt, tools, sky. Blink: just the field again. Dizziness passing but returning.
Bold text on dark: 'You don't mention it. You do remember.'
Evening: standing in yard. Warm heavy air. Across road someone pulls their shirt. Just for a second. Adjusting.
Same place on body. Same curve visible for one second across the road. Shirt pulled back immediately.
Neither looks at the other. Both know without looking. Shared scar. Shared silence. Recognized without acknowledging.
Bold text on dark: 'You don't need to.'
Vehicle stopping near their house next morning. Unknown. Different province plates. Nobody exits. Nobody enters.
Vehicle just waits. Then leaves. Stickman closing door. System continuing. Not wanting to think about it.
Door closed. Not liking thinking about any of this. But thinking. Silence on dirt road continuing.
Mirror of HOOK: stickman pulling shirt down. Scar still there underneath. Same gesture. Warm amber edges.
Bold text on dark: 'It didn't grow back. Nothing did.'
Bold text on black: 'Somewhere behind you, a door opens.'
Bold text on dark fading: 'Or you think it does.'
